commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Missing an icon/emoji for the current weather condition as explicitly requested in the prompt.
issues.fix: Add an icon or emoji alongside the current condition text (e.g., accepting a new `currentIcon` prop or mapping the condition string to an emoji).

issues.description: The wrapper div has an `aria-label` but lacks a semantic `role`, meaning screen readers may ignore the label.
issues.fix: Add `role="region"` or `role="group"` to the div containing the aria-label.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, visually clean, and handles missing props gracefully with sensible fallbacks. However, it misses the requested icon for the current weather condition, and the accessibility label on the wrapper div requires a semantic role to be announced reliably.

evaluation.critique: Strong component with proper use of design tokens and semantic color variables throughout. Main weaknesses: lacks hover/transition polish on forecast items, missing loading/empty states for when forecast array is empty, and could benefit from subtle animations when data updates.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: The current weather condition text does not include an appropriate icon/emoji as requested in the prompt.
issues.fix: Map the current condition string to an emoji and display it alongside or above the condition text.

issues.description: Emojis used for the forecast icons do not have proper accessibility attributes.
issues.fix: Wrap emojis in a span with role="img" and a descriptive aria-label (e.g., aria-label={day.condition}).

evaluations.critique: The component is visually very clean, well-structured, and correctly maps all provided props to a polished layout. However, it misses the current weather icon/emoji requirement and could improve accessibility by properly labeling the emojis for screen readers.

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates excellent adherence to design tokens and proper data handling from props with no hardcoded weather data in the component body. However, it lacks interactive polish (no hover/focus states on forecast items or detail pills) and could benefit from subtle transitions when data updates.

issues.description: Using Math.random() for React keys causes components to unnecessarily unmount and remount on every re-render.
issues.fix: Use the array index as a fallback key if atomicNumber is not available.

issues.description: Directly mutating e.currentTarget.style in onMouseEnter/onFocus is a React anti-pattern. Changes can be wiped out on re-renders.
issues.fix: Track hover/focus states using React state, or use standard CSS pseudo-classes if the environment allows.

issues.description: The colors array is recreated on every invocation of getCategoryColor, which is called inside a map loop.
issues.fix: Move the colors array declaration outside of the component or function scope.

evaluations.critique: The component does an excellent job fulfilling all requirements with a solid visual layout and functional filtering/selection. However, the code quality suffers from notable React anti-patterns, specifically directly mutating DOM styles for interactivity and using random numbers for keys.

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates solid implementation with proper CSS Grid for the periodic table, good use of design tokens, and strong interactive polish with hover/focus states. Minor issues include incomplete final Card section in the code snippet (appears truncated), and the detail panel uses inline `<strong>` styling instead of semantic text weight props, but overall structure and data handling are excellent.
